analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes CyberArk Conjur authentication and secrets management logs, transforming them into standardized OCSF format. It primarily handles authentication events, user access attempts, and secrets management operations, with specific focus on mapping authentication metadata and user activities.

factors: Complex field mappings for authentication data

optimization_opportunities: Add error handling for timestamp parsing
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: cyberark_conjur-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Authentication (3002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:16:50.691236

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format
local pcall = pcall

-- ISO8601 to epoch converter
local function iso8601_to_epoch(iso_str)
    if not iso_str then return nil end
    local pattern = "(%d+)%-(%d+)%-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)"
    local y,m,d,h,min,s = iso_str:match(pattern)
    if not y then return nil end
    return os.time({year=y, month=m, day=d, hour=h, min=min, sec=s})
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with detailed error messages
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record structure"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with constant fields
    local output = {
        -- Classification 
        activity_id = 1,
        activity_name = "Logon",
        category_uid = 3,
        category_name = "Identity & Access Management",
        class_uid = 3002,
        class_name = "Authentication",
        type_uid = 300201,
        type_name = "Authentication: Logon",
        
        -- Metadata
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Conjur Secrets Management",
                vendor_name = "CyberArk"
            },
            version = "1.0.0"
        }
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    local status, time = pcall(function()
        if record.timestamp then
            local epoch = iso8601_to_epoch(record.timestamp)
            if epoch then
                return epoch
            end
        end
        return os_time()
    end)
    output.time = status and time or os_time()

    -- Efficient field mappings using local references
    if record.hostname then 
        output.src_endpoint = {hostname = record.hostname}
    end
    
    if record.operation then
        output.activity_name = record.operation
    end
    
    if record.result then
        output.status = record.result
    end
    
    if record.role then
        output.actor = {user = {name = record.role}}
    end

    -- Additional field mappings with validation
    local field_mappings = {
        {src = "authenticator", dst = "auth_protocol"},
        {src = "user", dst = "user.name"},
        {src = "resource_id", dst = "resource.uid"},
        {src = "secret_id", dst = "resource.name"},
        {src = "privileges", dst = "privileges"},
        {src = "message", dst = "message"}
    }

    -- Batch process field mappings
    for _, mapping in ipairs(field_mappings) do
        if record[mapping.src] then
            local value = record[mapping.src]
            if type(value) == "string" and #value > 0 then
                -- Handle nested field paths
                local target = output
                for field in mapping.dst:gmatch("[^%.]+") do
                    if field ~= mapping.dst:match("[^%.]+$") then
                        target[field] = target[field] or {}
                        target = target[field]
                    else
                        target[field] = value
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    end

    -- Final validation of required OCSF fields
    if not output.time or not output.class_uid then
        return nil, "Missing required OCSF fields"
    end

    return output
end
